original_hypothesis.title: LockingMultiRewards.recover Can Drain Reward Token Balances Without Accounting Update
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recover() function can transfer any token except excess staking tokens. For reward tokens, there is no check against the amount needed for pending/accrued rewards. The owner can call recover(rewardToken, fullBalance) to drain all reward tokens, making it impossible for stakers to claim their earned rewards. The reward accounting (rewardRate, rewardPerTokenStored) is not updated, creating phantom rewards that can never be collected.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: recover() only has special handling for stakingToken. All other tokens including reward tokens can be fully withdrawn by the owner.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LockingMultiRewards.s.sol shows ownership transferred to safe.ops multisig

proof_of_signal.pattern: recover checks only stakingToken balance; reward tokens transferable in full; no accounting update for reward pools

preconditions: Reward tokens have been distributed via notifyRewardAmount

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Owner calls recover(rewardToken, balance) draining all reward tokens; stakers' earned rewards become uncollectable

operational_questions: Is the owner a multisig with appropriate security?
What is the total value of reward tokens across all LockingMultiRewards instances?
Is there monitoring for large recover() calls?
validation_requirements: Verify owner can recover reward tokens in full
Verify staker reward claims revert after drain

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All reward tokens withdrawn to owner
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Stakers' earned rewards become phantom balances - _getRewards transfers revert
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: No way to restore without re-funding

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete loss of distributed but unclaimed rewards
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All reward claims revert; stakers cannot access earned rewards
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Owner must re-fund reward tokens to restore functionality

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Large recover() calls for reward tokens
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Reward claim failures after owner action

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
